metadata.title: Awards, Honours & Persons in News Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Which organization won the Gold Prize for Public Outreach and Communication at the India International Trade Fair (IITF) 2025?

options.label: C
options.text: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs

answer.label: C
answer.text: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs

questions.explanation: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) won the Gold Prize for Public Outreach and Communication at the India International Trade Fair (IITF) 2025 in New Delhi. The award recognised its innovative, inclusive, and people-friendly communication through the GST and Customs Pavilion. The pavilion ran from 14–27 November 2025 with the theme “Next-Gen Goods and Services Tax (GST): Simple Tax, Prosperous Nation.”

questions.question: Which Indian woman received the 8th Dr. Paulos Mar Gregorios Award 2025 for excellence in science and women empowerment?
questions.explanation: Dr. Tessy Thomas, called the Missile Woman of India, received the 8th Dr. Paulos Mar Gregorios Award 2025 for excellence in science and women empowerment. She is a senior scientist at the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO). She was the first Indian woman to lead a missile project and headed Agni-IV and Agni-V ballistic missile programmes. She is also known as “Agniputri”, meaning one born of fire. The award is given by the Sophia Society of the Malankara Orthodox Church in memory of Dr. Paulos Mar Gregorios.

questions.question: Which Indian oficial has assumed the chairship of International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance for 2026?
questions.explanation: Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) Gyanesh Kumar assumes the chairship of the International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA) for 2026. India is elected as Chair under the rotational system and will hold the post till 2033. India is among the 14 founding member states of International IDEA. The charge is assumed in Stockholm, Sweden, in the presence of Ambassador Anurag Bhushan. The Election Commission of India (ECI) is globally recognised as a credible Election Management Body (EMB). India has 900+ million voters across 28 States and 8 Union Territories (UTs).

questions.question: Who has become the youngest and the first Asian to receive the ‘Global Humanitarian Award’?
questions.explanation: Anant Ambani, founder of Vantara, received the Global Humanitarian Award in the United States for his outstanding contributions to wildlife conservation and animal welfare. He was honoured for his leadership in the rescue, treatment, rehabilitation, and conservation of animals through the Vantara initiative. Anant Ambani is the youngest and the first Asian to ever receive this prestigious award. The award has brought global recognition to Vantara, which is considered one of the world’s largest and most unique wildlife conservation projects.

questions.question: Which Indian bureaucrat won 2025 UN Champions of the Earth Award?
questions.explanation: UN Environment Programme (UNEP) awarded IAS officer Supriya Sahu the 2025 UN Champions of the Earth award in the Inspiration and Action category. She was honoured for her long-standing leadership in plastic reduction, wildlife conservation, and especially sustainable cooling. Her key initiative, Tamil Nadu’s Cool Roof Project, lowered indoor temperatures by 5–8°C using high Solar Reflective Index paint, offering a low-cost cooling solution. UNEP called it a game changer for cities facing extreme heat, showing scalable climate action.

questions.question: Dinesh Khatak, who recently received the “Kendriya Grah Mantri Dakshata Padak,” is an officer of which force?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has awarded the “Kendriya Grah Mantri Dakshata Padak” (Union Home Minister’s Efficiency Medal) to Dinesh Khatak, Second-in-Command (2-IC) of Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) 196 Battalion, for his exceptional service, discipline, and contributions to national security. A native of Intel Kalan village in Jind district, Dinesh joined the CRPF as a Gazetted Officer in 2005 after clearing the UPSC exam. He served in Jammu & Kashmir during 2008–13 and 2017–21, contributing to Amarnath Yatra security, law and order, Article 370 abrogation duties, and COVID-19 pandemic operations.

questions.question: “The One: Cricket, My Life and More” is an autobiography of which Indian cricketer?
questions.explanation: Indian cricketer Shikhar Dhawan launched his autobiography The One: Cricket, My Life and More at an event in New Delhi. The launch highlighted how mindset and manifestation shaped his cricket career and life decisions. The book reflects principles that guide his venture Da One Group, which focuses on structured athlete development and grassroots sports. He also linked the book’s philosophy to the Shikhar Dhawan Foundation, working in education and community development.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the new Chief Information Commissioner (CIC) of India?
questions.explanation: Retired Indian Administrative Service officer Raj Kumar Goyal was sworn in as the Chief Information Commissioner of the Central Information Commission. President Droupadi Murmu administered the oath of office at Rashtrapati Bhavan. Raj Kumar Goyal is a 1990-batch Indian Administrative Service officer. He retired as Secretary in the Department of Justice under the Ministry of Law and Justice. He also served as Secretary for Border Management in the Ministry of Home Affairs. He succeeded Heeralal Samariya as Chief Information Commissioner. His appointment was made under Section 12 of the Right to Information Act, 2005, and took effect from December 15.

questions.question: Which organisation under the Ministry of Culture won two Public Relations Society of India (PRSI) National Awards in 2025?
questions.explanation: The National Council of Science Museums (NCSM), under the Ministry of Culture, won two PRSI National Awards 2025 from the Public Relations Society of India (PRSI). One award was for the Har Ghar Museum initiative in the Best Use of Social Media in a Corporate Campaign category. The second award was for the “Waste to Art” publication under the Special/Prestige Publication category. “Waste to Art” documents NCSM’s initiative under Special Campaign 4.0 and Swachhata Hi Sewa, transforming around 1,250 kg of waste into art installations across NCSM units. The installations served as educational exhibits promoting circular economy principles, and workshops and competitions engaged students and communities.

questions.question: Which airport has been named the best international airport in the world for 2025 at India’s Best Awards?
questions.explanation: Singapore Changi Airport has been named the best international airport in the world for 2025 at India’s Best Awards by Travel+Leisure (T+L). The airport is known for sky-lit interiors, indoor gardens, art-filled spaces, and attractions like the world’s tallest indoor waterfall and butterfly gardens. The award was part of the 14th India’s Best Awards, reflecting traveller preferences through online voting. Changi Airport sets global benchmarks by combining efficiency, comfort, and design, making it a destination in itself.
